---
subject: "What I’ve been reading"
from: "Marginal REVOLUTION <donotreply@wordpress.com>"
to: ""
date: 2025-10-03 05:23:57
labels: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Tyler Cowen"]
label_ids: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Label_2466199532210586699"]
---
Marcus Willaschek, [Kant: A Revolution in Thinking](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=586547b6cd017bbfd7671108f6ac142775fe460a15e77b10748b6860f1eb889d&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=a5f43082e0906be55298a8adf1df8479&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d). A very good book, perhaps the best introduction to Kant? Though for me it is mostly interior to my current knowledge set.
Matthew Bell, [Goethe: A Life in Ideas](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=ba76993ed4a606568a8e7144c028618161a51a6711f2581a26ddaa1cac03fc7d&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=0a3d39f0d5799cf30ccdfd4d07520d15&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d). A beautiful book, now in English we have Nicholas Boyle's work and also this. Bell is wise enough to understand and value Iphigenia auf Tauris, a good test for Goethe appeciation. Although I had a library copy out to read, I went ahead and bought a copy of this one to own.
Benjamin Wilson, [Strange Stability: How Cold War Scientists Set Out to Control the Arms Race and Ended up Serving the Military-Industrial Complex](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=c0c87efef19e6c1d4df1f3a5564458d7cbf9e20f11acf043de7b68b1417536ea&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=694945f8864975f32d793125aa97e60a&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d) is both interesting and has plenty of information on early Thomas Schelling and his precursors.
Very well researched is [The Highest Exam: How the Gaokao Shapes China](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=5325255382175b4ca821c573e37b6ac1debdca5eebe25a096f9195d6409967e2&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=f89a0817f046435bb6db84d6c93bb458&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d), by Ruixue Jia and Hongbin Li, with Claire Cousineau.
Peter Baxter, [Rhodesia: A Complete History 1890-1980](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=497d2fd93bef05e9bb67a904aadaa367a72c803f417a0a6e23b65402b8b559ba&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=8275854b7a8029719a016910727bb1e3&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d). The most complete history of the country I have been able to find. Many of the other books contain a few dominant, non-false narratives, but one gets tired of that? I say LLMs come especially in handy for learning this history.
Luka Ivan Jukic, [Central Europe: The Death of a Civilization and the Life of an Idea](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=68cbdba7316872d478018e288fb32d522a3e424c085f6a0e0d909d897385957c&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=bb14bb644b1bfedefb1f0f1ab1afe15d&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d). I took this sentence to encapsulate the main lesson of the book, namely that this does not usually work: "Central Europeans were, as ever, masterfully adept at rearranging polities into new configurations."
I enjoyed Maxim Samson, [Earth Shapers: How We Mapped and Mastered the World, From the Panama Canal to the Baltic Way](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=9305be66f8c2df19170b99b293cb5d1d675a3663c4ed3dbe10fcc0f493503645&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91553&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=cd034396ad55ecb028f348c66ba5b078&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=234abe8e8d0528afecf8d38e1b9da51d).
